rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERTRUDE LEE HUGHES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Set One Admitted And Conclusively Established; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Margaret E. Coleman And James Mills
rulings.Rulings: Thomas Otto Coleman's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents is granted. Ronald Coleman filed no opposition to the motion to compel. Sanctions in the amount of $950.00 are awarded. Ronald Coleman must serve responses without objections no later than May 16, 2014. Failure to comply with this order could result in further sanctions, including evidentiary and/or terminating sanctions. The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling should call the Court no later than 4/30/2014, at 1;00 p.m. and notify opposing counsel.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE INNOCENTE CASSOLATO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice And Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena For Consumer Financial Records Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order Allowing A Redaction Of The Requested Records, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: The parties are ordered to meet and confer prior to the hearing on the following issues: (1) What banking account information (checks, etc.) remains to be provided given Jean's accounting in November 2013? (2) Can any information (not previously provided in the accounting) be redacted in such a way to allay any privacy concerns? (3) Do the parties stipulate that the accounts owned by Innocente Cassolate and Carol Cassolato are subject to the subpoena, and the only account(s) at issue are Jean's? After the parties meet and confer, should they not have a stipulated order that resolves the motion to quash, they shall appear for argument on any remaining issues.
